stemHtml:
Refer to figure or STUTTGART (EDDS) chart 11-1 from Jeppesen GSPRM 2017.
A pilot is under radar vectors in order to position for an ILS approach to runway 07. ATC clears the aircraft to descend to 4000 ft on a heading of 105° to establish on and follow the ILS. Suddenly a complete electrical failure occurs and all navigation instruments are lost except for the standby compass. If the aircraft is in cloud, what is a safe altitude for the pilot to maintain while trying to restore the navigation instruments?

options.a: 1467 ft as this is the minima for the CAT I ILS being flown.
options.b: 3300 ft as the aircraft is probably within 15 NM of STG VOR.
options.c: 4000 ft as this is the platform altitude for the ILS approach.
options.d: 4600 ft as this is the highest MSA around the airport.
